ГўВҖВңBut, sir,ГўВҖВқ remarked the young woman, ГўВҖВңthese Eastern societies, in the
midst of which you have passed a portion of your existence, are as
fantastic as the tales that come from their strange land. A man can
easily be put out of the way there, then; it is, indeed, the Bagdad and
Bassora of the _Thousand and One Nights_. The sultans and viziers who
rule over society there, and who constitute what in France we call the
government, are really Haroun-al-Raschids and Giaffars, who not only
pardon a poisoner, but even make him a prime minister, if his crime has
been an ingenious one, and who, under such circumstances, have the
whole story written in letters of gold, to divert their hours of
idleness and _ennui_.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBy no means, madame; the fanciful exists no longer in the East. There,
disguised under other names, and concealed under other costumes, are
police agents, magistrates, attorneys-general, and bailiffs. They hang,
behead, and impale their criminals in the most agreeable possible
manner; but some of these, like clever rogues, have contrived to escape
human justice, and succeed in their fraudulent enterprises by cunning
stratagems. Amongst us a simpleton, possessed by the demon of hate or
cupidity, who has an enemy to destroy, or some near relation to dispose
of, goes straight to the grocerГўВҖВҷs or druggistГўВҖВҷs, gives a false name,
which leads more easily to his detection than his real one, and under
the pretext that the rats prevent him from sleeping, purchases five or
six grammes of arsenicГўВҖВ”if he is really a cunning fellow, he goes to
five or six different druggists or grocers, and thereby becomes only
five or six times more easily traced;ГўВҖВ”then, when he has acquired his
specific, he administers duly to his enemy, or near kinsman, a dose of
arsenic which would make a mammoth or mastodon burst, and which,
without rhyme or reason, makes his victim utter groans which alarm the
entire neighborhood. Then arrive a crowd of policemen and constables.
They fetch a doctor, who opens the dead body, and collects from the
entrails and stomach a quantity of arsenic in a spoon. Next day a
hundred newspapers relate the fact, with the names of the victim and
the murderer. The same evening the grocer or grocers, druggist or
druggists, come and say, ГўВҖВҳIt was I who sold the arsenic to the
gentleman;ГўВҖВҷ and rather than not recognize the guilty purchaser, they
will recognize twenty. Then the foolish criminal is taken, imprisoned,
interrogated, confronted, confounded, condemned, and cut off by hemp or
steel; or if she be a woman of any consideration, they lock her up for
life. This is the way in which you Northerns understand chemistry,
madame. Desrues was, however, I must confess, more skilful.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat would you have, sir?ГўВҖВқ said the lady, laughing; ГўВҖВңwe do what we
can. All the world has not the secret of the Medicis or the Borgias.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNow,ГўВҖВқ replied the count, shrugging his shoulders, ГўВҖВңshall I tell you
the cause of all these stupidities? It is because, at your theatres, by
what at least I could judge by reading the pieces they play, they see
persons swallow the contents of a phial, or suck the button of a ring,
and fall dead instantly. Five minutes afterwards the curtain falls, and
the spectators depart. They are ignorant of the consequences of the
murder; they see neither the police commissary with his badge of
office, nor the corporal with his four men; and so the poor fools
believe that the whole thing is as easy as lying. But go a little way
from FranceГўВҖВ”go either to Aleppo or Cairo, or only to Naples or Rome,
and you will see people passing by you in the streetsГўВҖВ”people erect,
smiling, and fresh-colored, of whom Asmodeus, if you were holding on by
the skirt of his mantle, would say, ГўВҖВҳThat man was poisoned three weeks
ago; he will be a dead man in a month.ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen,ГўВҖВқ remarked Madame de Villefort, ГўВҖВңthey have again discovered the
secret of the famous _aqua Tofana_ that they said was lost at Perugia.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, but madame, does mankind ever lose anything? The arts change about
and make a tour of the world; things take a different name, and the
vulgar do not follow themГўВҖВ”that is all; but there is always the same
result. Poisons act particularly on some organ or anotherГўВҖВ”one on the
stomach, another on the brain, another on the intestines. Well, the
poison brings on a cough, the cough an inflammation of the lungs, or
some other complaint catalogued in the book of science, which, however,
by no means precludes it from being decidedly mortal; and if it were
not, would be sure to become so, thanks to the remedies applied by
foolish doctors, who are generally bad chemists, and which will act in
favor of or against the malady, as you please; and then there is a
human being killed according to all the rules of art and skill, and of
whom justice learns nothing, as was said by a terrible chemist of my
acquaintance, the worthy AbbГғВ© Adelmonte of Taormina, in Sicily, who has
studied these national phenomena very profoundly.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt is quite frightful, but deeply interesting,ГўВҖВқ said the young lady,
motionless with attention. ГўВҖВңI thought, I must confess, that these
tales, were inventions of the Middle Ages.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, no doubt, but improved upon by ours. What is the use of time,
rewards of merit, medals, crosses, Monthyon prizes, if they do not lead
society towards more complete perfection? Yet man will never be perfect
until he learns to create and destroy; he does know how to destroy, and
that is half the battle.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSo,ГўВҖВқ added Madame de Villefort, constantly returning to her object,
ГўВҖВңthe poisons of the Borgias, the Medicis, the RenГғВ©es, the Ruggieris,
and later, probably, that of Baron de Trenck, whose story has been so
misused by modern drama and romanceГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWere objects of art, madame, and nothing more,ГўВҖВқ replied the count. ГўВҖВңDo
you suppose that the real _savant_ addresses himself stupidly to the
mere individual? By no means. Science loves eccentricities, leaps and
bounds, trials of strength, fancies, if I may be allowed so to term
them. Thus, for instance, the excellent AbbГғВ© Adelmonte, of whom I spoke
just now, made in this way some marvellous experiments.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңReally?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes; I will mention one to you. He had a remarkably fine garden, full
of vegetables, flowers, and fruit. From amongst these vegetables he
selected the most simpleГўВҖВ”a cabbage, for instance. For three days he
watered this cabbage with a distillation of arsenic; on the third, the
cabbage began to droop and turn yellow. At that moment he cut it. In
the eyes of everybody it seemed fit for table, and preserved its
wholesome appearance. It was only poisoned to the AbbГғВ© Adelmonte. He
then took the cabbage to the room where he had rabbitsГўВҖВ”for the AbbГғВ©
Adelmonte had a collection of rabbits, cats, and guinea-pigs, fully as
fine as his collection of vegetables, flowers, and fruit. Well, the
AbbГғВ© Adelmonte took a rabbit, and made it eat a leaf of the cabbage.
The rabbit died. What magistrate would find, or even venture to
insinuate, anything against this? What procureur has ever ventured to
draw up an accusation against M. Magendie or M. Flourens, in
consequence of the rabbits, cats, and guinea-pigs they have killed?ГўВҖВ”not
one. So, then, the rabbit dies, and justice takes no notice. This
rabbit dead, the AbbГғВ© Adelmonte has its entrails taken out by his cook
and thrown on the dunghill; on this dunghill is a hen, who, pecking
these intestines, is in her turn taken ill, and dies next day. At the
moment when she is struggling in the convulsions of death, a vulture is
flying by (there are a good many vultures in AdelmonteГўВҖВҷs country); this
bird darts on the dead fowl, and carries it away to a rock, where it
dines off its prey. Three days afterwards, this poor vulture, which has
been very much indisposed since that dinner, suddenly feels very giddy
while flying aloft in the clouds, and falls heavily into a fish-pond.
The pike, eels, and carp eat greedily always, as everybody knowsГўВҖВ”well,
they feast on the vulture. Now suppose that next day, one of these
eels, or pike, or carp, poisoned at the fourth remove, is served up at
your table. Well, then, your guest will be poisoned at the fifth
remove, and die, at the end of eight or ten days, of pains in the
intestines, sickness, or abscess of the pylorus. The doctors open the
body and say with an air of profound learning, ГўВҖВҳThe subject has died of
a tumor on the liver, or of typhoid fever!ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut,ГўВҖВқ remarked Madame de Villefort, ГўВҖВңall these circumstances which you
link thus to one another may be broken by the least accident; the
vulture may not see the fowl, or may fall a hundred yards from the
fish-pond.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, that is where the art comes in. To be a great chemist in the East,
one must direct chance; and this is to be achieved.ГўВҖВқ

Madame de Villefort was in deep thought, yet listened attentively.

ГўВҖВңBut,ГўВҖВқ she exclaimed, suddenly, ГўВҖВңarsenic is indelible, indestructible;
in whatsoever way it is absorbed, it will be found again in the body of
the victim from the moment when it has been taken in sufficient
quantity to cause death.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңPrecisely so,ГўВҖВқ cried Monte CristoГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВңprecisely so; and this is what I
said to my worthy Adelmonte. He reflected, smiled, and replied to me by
a Sicilian proverb, which I believe is also a French proverb, ГўВҖВҳMy son,
the world was not made in a dayГўВҖВ”but in seven. Return on Sunday.ГўВҖВҷ On the
Sunday following I did return to him. Instead of having watered his
cabbage with arsenic, he had watered it this time with a solution of
salts, having their basis in strychnine, _strychnos colubrina_, as the
learned term it. Now, the cabbage had not the slightest appearance of
disease in the world, and the rabbit had not the smallest distrust;
yet, five minutes afterwards, the rabbit was dead. The fowl pecked at
the rabbit, and the next day was a dead hen. This time we were the
vultures; so we opened the bird, and this time all special symptoms had
disappeared, there were only general symptoms. There was no peculiar
indication in any organГўВҖВ”an excitement of the nervous systemГўВҖВ”that was
it; a case of cerebral congestionГўВҖВ”nothing more. The fowl had not been
poisonedГўВҖВ”she had died of apoplexy. Apoplexy is a rare disease among
fowls, I believe, but very common among men.ГўВҖВқ

Madame de Villefort appeared more and more thoughtful.

ГўВҖВңIt is very fortunate,ГўВҖВқ she observed, ГўВҖВңthat such substances could only
be prepared by chemists; otherwise, all the world would be poisoning
each other.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBy chemists and persons who have a taste for chemistry,ГўВҖВқ said Monte
Cristo carelessly.

ГўВҖВңAnd then,ГўВҖВқ said Madame de Villefort, endeavoring by a struggle, and
with effort, to get away from her thoughts, ГўВҖВңhowever skilfully it is
prepared, crime is always crime, and if it avoid human scrutiny, it
does not escape the eye of God. The Orientals are stronger than we are
in cases of conscience, and, very prudently, have no hellГўВҖВ”that is the
point.ГўВҖВқ

30075m



ГўВҖВңReally, madame, this is a scruple which naturally must occur to a pure
mind like yours, but which would easily yield before sound reasoning.
The bad side of human thought will always be defined by the paradox of
Jean Jacques Rousseau,ГўВҖВ”you remember,ГўВҖВ”the mandarin who is killed five
hundred leagues off by raising the tip of the finger. ManГўВҖВҷs whole life
passes in doing these things, and his intellect is exhausted by
reflecting on them. You will find very few persons who will go and
brutally thrust a knife in the heart of a fellow-creature, or will
administer to him, in order to remove him from the surface of the globe
on which we move with life and animation, that quantity of arsenic of
which we just now talked. Such a thing is really out of ruleГўВҖВ”eccentric
or stupid. To attain such a point, the blood must be heated to
thirty-six degrees, the pulse be, at least, at ninety, and the feelings
excited beyond the ordinary limit. But suppose one pass, as is
permissible in philology, from the word itself to its softened synonym,
then, instead of committing an ignoble assassination you make an
ГўВҖВҳelimination;ГўВҖВҷ you merely and simply remove from your path the
individual who is in your way, and that without shock or violence,
without the display of the sufferings which, in the case of becoming a
punishment, make a martyr of the victim, and a butcher, in every sense
of the word, of him who inflicts them. Then there will be no blood, no
groans, no convulsions, and above all, no consciousness of that horrid
and compromising moment of accomplishing the act,ГўВҖВ”then one escapes the
clutch of the human law, which says, ГўВҖВҳDo not disturb society!ГўВҖВҷ This is
the mode in which they manage these things, and succeed in Eastern
climes, where there are grave and phlegmatic persons who care very
little for the questions of time in conjunctures of importance.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYet conscience remains,ГўВҖВқ remarked Madame de Villefort in an agitated
voice, and with a stifled sigh.

ГўВҖВңYes,ГўВҖВқ answered Monte Cristo ГўВҖВңhappily, yes, conscience does remain; and
if it did not, how wretched we should be! After every action requiring
exertion, it is conscience that saves us, for it supplies us with a
thousand good excuses, of which we alone are judges; and these reasons,
howsoever excellent in producing sleep, would avail us but very little
before a tribunal, when we were tried for our lives. Thus Richard III.,
for instance, was marvellously served by his conscience after the
putting away of the two children of Edward IV.; in fact, he could say,
ГўВҖВҳThese two children of a cruel and persecuting king, who have inherited
the vices of their father, which I alone could perceive in their
juvenile propensitiesГўВҖВ”these two children are impediments in my way of
promoting the happiness of the English people, whose unhappiness they
(the children) would infallibly have caused.ГўВҖВҷ Thus was Lady Macbeth
served by her conscience, when she sought to give her son, and not her
husband (whatever Shakespeare may say), a throne. Ah, maternal love is
a great virtue, a powerful motiveГўВҖВ”so powerful that it excuses a
multitude of things, even if, after DuncanГўВҖВҷs death, Lady Macbeth had
been at all pricked by her conscience.ГўВҖВқ

Madame de Villefort listened with avidity to these appalling maxims and
horrible paradoxes, delivered by the count with that ironical
simplicity which was peculiar to him.

After a momentГўВҖВҷs silence, the lady inquired:

ГўВҖВңDo you know, my dear count,ГўВҖВқ she said, ГўВҖВңthat you are a very terrible
reasoner, and that you look at the world through a somewhat distempered
medium? Have you really measured the world by scrutinies, or through
alembics and crucibles? For you must indeed be a great chemist, and the
elixir you administered to my son, which recalled him to life almost
instantaneouslyГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВқ

30077m



ГўВҖВңOh, do not place any reliance on that, madame; _one_ drop of that
elixir sufficed to recall life to a dying child, but three drops would
have impelled the blood into his lungs in such a way as to have
produced most violent palpitations; six would have suspended his
respiration, and caused syncope more serious than that in which he was;
ten would have destroyed him. You know, madame, how suddenly I snatched
him from those phials which he so imprudently touched?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIs it then so terrible a poison?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, no! In the first place, let us agree that the word poison does not
exist, because in medicine use is made of the most violent poisons,
which become, according as they are employed, most salutary remedies.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat, then, is it?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA skilful preparation of my friendГўВҖВҷs the worthy AbbГғВ© Adelmonte, who
taught me the use of it.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh,ГўВҖВқ observed Madame de Villefort, ГўВҖВңit must be an admirable
anti-spasmodic.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңPerfect, madame, as you have seen,ГўВҖВқ replied the count; ГўВҖВңand I
frequently make use of itГўВҖВ”with all possible prudence though, be it
observed,ГўВҖВқ he added with a smile of intelligence.

ГўВҖВңMost assuredly,ГўВҖВқ responded Madame de Villefort in the same tone. ГўВҖВңAs
for me, so nervous, and so subject to fainting fits, I should require a
Doctor Adelmonte to invent for me some means of breathing freely and
tranquillizing my mind, in the fear I have of dying some fine day of
suffocation. In the meanwhile, as the thing is difficult to find in
France, and your abbГғВ© is not probably disposed to make a journey to
Paris on my account, I must continue to use Monsieur PlancheГўВҖВҷs
anti-spasmodics; and mint and HoffmanГўВҖВҷs drops are among my favorite
remedies. Here are some lozenges which I have made up on purpose; they
are compounded doubly strong.ГўВҖВқ

Monte Cristo opened the tortoise-shell box, which the lady presented to
him, and inhaled the odor of the lozenges with the air of an amateur
who thoroughly appreciated their composition.

ГўВҖВңThey are indeed exquisite,ГўВҖВқ he said; ГўВҖВңbut as they are necessarily
submitted to the process of deglutitionГўВҖВ”a function which it is
frequently impossible for a fainting person to accomplishГўВҖВ”I prefer my
own specific.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңUndoubtedly, and so should I prefer it, after the effects I have seen
produced; but of course it is a secret, and I am not so indiscreet as
to ask it of you.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut I,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo, rising as he spokeГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВңI am gallant enough to
offer it you.ГўВҖВқ

30079m



ГўВҖВңHow kind you are.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOnly remember one thingГўВҖВ”a small dose is a remedy, a large one is
poison. One drop will restore life, as you have seen; five or six will
inevitably kill, and in a way the more terrible inasmuch as, poured
into a glass of wine, it would not in the slightest degree affect its
flavor. But I say no more, madame; it is really as if I were
prescribing for you.ГўВҖВқ

The clock struck half-past six, and a lady was announced, a friend of
Madame de Villefort, who came to dine with her.

ГўВҖВңIf I had had the honor of seeing you for the third or fourth time,
count, instead of only for the second,ГўВҖВқ said Madame de Villefort; ГўВҖВңif I
had had the honor of being your friend, instead of only having the
happiness of being under an obligation to you, I should insist on
detaining you to dinner, and not allow myself to be daunted by a first
refusal.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA thousand thanks, madame,ГўВҖВқ replied Monte Cristo ГўВҖВңbut I have an
engagement which I cannot break. I have promised to escort to the
AcadГғВ©mie a Greek princess of my acquaintance who has never seen your
grand opera, and who relies on me to conduct her thither.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAdieu, then, sir, and do not forget the prescription.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, in truth, madame, to do that I must forget the hourГўВҖВҷs conversation
I have had with you, which is indeed impossible.ГўВҖВқ

Monte Cristo bowed, and left the house. Madame de Villefort remained
immersed in thought.

ГўВҖВңHe is a very strange man,ГўВҖВқ she said, ГўВҖВңand in my opinion is himself the
Adelmonte he talks about.ГўВҖВқ

As to Monte Cristo the result had surpassed his utmost expectations.

ГўВҖВңGood,ГўВҖВқ said he, as he went away; ГўВҖВңthis is a fruitful soil, and I feel
certain that the seed sown will not be cast on barren ground.ГўВҖВқ

Next morning, faithful to his promise, he sent the prescription
requested.



 Chapter 53. Robert le Diable

The pretext of an opera engagement was so much the more feasible, as
there chanced to be on that very night a more than ordinary attraction
at the AcadГғВ©mie Royale. Levasseur, who had been suffering under severe
illness, made his reappearance in the character of _Bertram_, and, as
usual, the announcement of the most admired production of the favorite
composer of the day had attracted a brilliant and fashionable audience.
Morcerf, like most other young men of rank and fortune, had his
orchestra stall, with the certainty of always finding a seat in at
least a dozen of the principal boxes occupied by persons of his
acquaintance; he had, moreover, his right of entry into the omnibus
box. ChГғВўteau-Renaud rented a stall beside his own, while Beauchamp, as
a journalist, had unlimited range all over the theatre. It happened
that on this particular night the ministerГўВҖВҷs box was placed at the
disposal of Lucien Debray, who offered it to the Comte de Morcerf, who
again, upon his rejection of it by MercГғВ©dГғВЁs, sent it to Danglars, with
an intimation that he should probably do himself the honor of joining
the baroness and her daughter during the evening, in the event of their
accepting the box in question. The ladies received the offer with too
much pleasure to dream of a refusal. To no class of persons is the
presentation of a gratuitous opera-box more acceptable than to the
wealthy millionaire, who still hugs economy while boasting of carrying
a kingГўВҖВҷs ransom in his waistcoat pocket.

Danglars had, however, protested against showing himself in a
ministerial box, declaring that his political principles, and his
parliamentary position as member of the opposition party would not
permit him so to commit himself; the baroness had, therefore,
despatched a note to Lucien Debray, bidding him call for them, it being
wholly impossible for her to go alone with EugГғВ©nie to the opera.

There is no gainsaying the fact that a very unfavorable construction
would have been put upon the circumstance if the two women had gone
without escort, while the addition of a third, in the person of her
motherГўВҖВҷs admitted lover, enabled Mademoiselle Danglars to defy malice
and ill-nature. One must take the world as one finds it.

30083m



The curtain rose, as usual, to an almost empty house, it being one of
the absurdities of Parisian fashion never to appear at the opera until
after the beginning of the performance, so that the first act is
generally played without the slightest attention being paid to it, that
part of the audience already assembled being too much occupied in
observing the fresh arrivals, while nothing is heard but the noise of
opening and shutting doors, and the buzz of conversation.

ГўВҖВңSurely,ГўВҖВқ said Albert, as the door of a box on the first circle opened,
ГўВҖВңthat must be the Countess GГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd who is the Countess GГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”?ГўВҖВқ inquired ChГғВўteau-Renaud.

ГўВҖВңWhat a question! Now, do you know, baron, I have a great mind to pick
a quarrel with you for asking it; as if all the world did not know who
the Countess GГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ” was.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, to be sure,ГўВҖВқ replied ChГғВўteau-Renaud; ГўВҖВңthe lovely Venetian, is it
not?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHerself.ГўВҖВқ At this moment the countess perceived Albert, and returned
his salutation with a smile.

ГўВҖВңYou know her, it seems?ГўВҖВқ said ChГғВўteau-Renaud.

ГўВҖВңFranz introduced me to her at Rome,ГўВҖВқ replied Albert.

ГўВҖВңWell, then, will you do as much for me in Paris as Franz did for you
in Rome?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWith pleasure.ГўВҖВқ

There was a cry of ГўВҖВңShut up!ГўВҖВқ from the audience. This manifestation on
the part of the spectators of their wish to be allowed to hear the
music, produced not the slightest effect on the two young men, who
continued their conversation.

ГўВҖВңThe countess was present at the races in the Champ-de-Mars,ГўВҖВқ said
ChГғВўteau-Renaud.

ГўВҖВңToday?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBless me, I quite forgot the races. Did you bet?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, merely a paltry fifty louis.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd who was the winner?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNautilus. I staked on him.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut there were three races, were there not?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes; there was the prize given by the Jockey ClubГўВҖВ”a gold cup, you
knowГўВҖВ”and a very singular circumstance occurred about that race.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat was it?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, shut up!ГўВҖВқ again interposed some of the audience.

ГўВҖВңWhy, it was won by a horse and rider utterly unknown on the course.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIs that possible?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңTrue as day. The fact was, nobody had observed a horse entered by the
name of Vampa, or that of a jockey styled Job, when, at the last
moment, a splendid roan, mounted by a jockey about as big as your fist,
presented themselves at the starting-post. They were obliged to stuff
at least twenty pounds weight of shot in the small riderГўВҖВҷs pockets, to
make him weight; but with all that he outstripped Ariel and Barbare,
against whom he ran, by at least three whole lengths.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd was it not found out at last to whom the horse and jockey
belonged?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYou say that the horse was entered under the name of Vampa?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңExactly; that was the title.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen,ГўВҖВқ answered Albert, ГўВҖВңI am better informed than you are, and know
who the owner of that horse was.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңShut up, there!ГўВҖВқ cried the pit in chorus. And this time the tone and
manner in which the command was given, betokened such growing hostility
that the two young men perceived, for the first time, that the mandate
was addressed to them. Leisurely turning round, they calmly scrutinized
the various countenances around them, as though demanding some one
person who would take upon himself the responsibility of what they
deemed excessive impertinence; but as no one responded to the
challenge, the friends turned again to the front of the theatre, and
affected to busy themselves with the stage. At this moment the door of
the ministerГўВҖВҷs box opened, and Madame Danglars, accompanied by her
daughter, entered, escorted by Lucien Debray, who assiduously conducted
them to their seats.

ГўВҖВңHa, ha,ГўВҖВқ said ChГғВўteau-Renaud, ГўВҖВңhere come some friends of yours,
viscount! What are you looking at there? donГўВҖВҷt you see they are trying
to catch your eye?ГўВҖВқ

Albert turned round, just in time to receive a gracious wave of the fan
from the baroness; as for Mademoiselle EugГғВ©nie, she scarcely vouchsafed
to waste the glances of her large black eyes even upon the business of
the stage.

ГўВҖВңI tell you what, my dear fellow,ГўВҖВқ said ChГғВўteau-Renaud, ГўВҖВңI cannot
imagine what objection you can possibly have to Mademoiselle
DanglarsГўВҖВ”that is, setting aside her want of ancestry and somewhat
inferior rank, which by the way I donГўВҖВҷt think you care very much about.
Now, barring all that, I mean to say she is a deuced fine girl!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHandsome, certainly,ГўВҖВқ replied Albert, ГўВҖВңbut not to my taste, which I
confess, inclines to something softer, gentler, and more feminine.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, well,ГўВҖВқ exclaimed ChГғВўteau-Renaud, who because he had seen his
thirtieth summer fancied himself duly warranted in assuming a sort of
paternal air with his more youthful friend, ГўВҖВңyou young people are never
satisfied; why, what would you have more? your parents have chosen you
a bride built on the model of Diana, the huntress, and yet you are not
content.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo, for that very resemblance affrights me; I should have liked
something more in the manner of the Venus of Milo or Capua; but this
chase-loving Diana, continually surrounded by her nymphs, gives me a
sort of alarm lest she should some day bring on me the fate of ActГғВҰon.ГўВҖВқ

And, indeed, it required but one glance at Mademoiselle Danglars to
comprehend the justness of MorcerfГўВҖВҷs remark. She was beautiful, but her
beauty was of too marked and decided a character to please a fastidious
taste; her hair was raven black, but its natural waves seemed somewhat
rebellious; her eyes, of the same color as her hair, were surmounted by
well-arched brows, whose great defect, however, consisted in an almost
habitual frown, while her whole physiognomy wore that expression of
firmness and decision so little in accordance with the gentler
attributes of her sexГўВҖВ”her nose was precisely what a sculptor would have
chosen for a chiselled Juno. Her mouth, which might have been found
fault with as too large, displayed teeth of pearly whiteness, rendered
still more conspicuous by the brilliant carmine of her lips,
contrasting vividly with her naturally pale complexion. But that which
completed the almost masculine look Morcerf found so little to his
taste, was a dark mole, of much larger dimensions than these freaks of
nature generally are, placed just at the corner of her mouth; and the
effect tended to increase the expression of self-dependence that
characterized her countenance.

The rest of Mademoiselle EugГғВ©nieГўВҖВҷs person was in perfect keeping with
the head just described; she, indeed, reminded one of Diana, as
ChГғВўteau-Renaud observed, but her bearing was more haughty and resolute.

As regarded her attainments, the only fault to be found with them was
the same that a fastidious connoisseur might have found with her
beauty, that they were somewhat too erudite and masculine for so young
a person. She was a perfect linguist, a first-rate artist, wrote
poetry, and composed music; to the study of the latter she professed to
be entirely devoted, following it with an indefatigable perseverance,
assisted by a schoolfellow,ГўВҖВ”a young woman without fortune whose talent
promised to develop into remarkable powers as a singer. It was rumored
that she was an object of almost paternal interest to one of the
principal composers of the day, who excited her to spare no pains in
the cultivation of her voice, which might hereafter prove a source of
wealth and independence. But this counsel effectually decided
Mademoiselle Danglars never to commit herself by being seen in public
with one destined for a theatrical life; and acting upon this
principle, the bankerГўВҖВҷs daughter, though perfectly willing to allow
Mademoiselle Louise dГўВҖВҷArmilly (that was the name of the young virtuosa)
to practice with her through the day, took especial care not to be seen
in her company. Still, though not actually received at the HГғВҙtel
Danglars in the light of an acknowledged friend, Louise was treated
with far more kindness and consideration than is usually bestowed on a
governess.

The curtain fell almost immediately after the entrance of Madame
Danglars into her box, the band quitted the orchestra for the
accustomed half-hourГўВҖВҷs interval allowed between the acts, and the
audience were left at liberty to promenade the salon or lobbies, or to
pay and receive visits in their respective boxes.

Morcerf and ChГғВўteau-Renaud were amongst the first to avail themselves

ГўВҖВңSo we meet again, my travelling friend, do we?ГўВҖВқ cried the countess,

merely from the circumstance of his being your attendantГўВҖВ”you, who are
at this moment the most celebrated and fashionable person in Paris.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңReally? and what has procured me so flattering a distinction?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat? why, yourself, to be sure! You give away horses worth a thousand
louis; you save the lives of ladies of high rank and beauty; under the
name of Major Black you run thoroughbreds ridden by tiny urchins not
larger than marmots; then, when you have carried off the golden trophy
of victory, instead of setting any value on it, you give it to the
first handsome woman you think of!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd who has filled your head with all this nonsense?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhy, in the first place, I heard it from Madame Danglars, who, by the
by, is dying to see you in her box, or to have you seen there by
others; secondly, I learned it from BeauchampГўВҖВҷs journal; and thirdly,
from my own imagination. Why, if you sought concealment, did you call
your horse Vampa?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat was an oversight, certainly,ГўВҖВқ replied the count; ГўВҖВңbut tell me,
does the Count of Morcerf never visit the Opera? I have been looking
for him, but without success.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHe will be here tonight.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIn what part of the house?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIn the baronessГўВҖВҷs box, I believe.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat charming young woman with her is her daughter?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI congratulate you.ГўВҖВқ

Morcerf smiled.

ГўВҖВңWe will discuss that subject at length some future time,ГўВҖВқ said he.
ГўВҖВңBut what do you think of the music?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat music?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhy, the music you have been listening to.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, it is well enough as the production of a human composer, sung by
featherless bipeds, to quote the late Diogenes.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңFrom which it would seem, my dear count, that you can at pleasure
enjoy the seraphic strains that proceed from the seven choirs of
paradise?ГўВҖВқ

30097m



ГўВҖВңYou are right, in some degree; when I wish to listen to sounds more
exquisitely attuned to melody than mortal ear ever yet listened to, I
go to sleep.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen sleep here, my dear count. The conditions are favorable; what
else was opera invented for?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo, thank you. Your orchestra is too noisy. To sleep after the manner
I speak of, absolute calm and silence are necessary, and then a certain
preparationГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI knowГўВҖВ”the famous hashish!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңPrecisely. So, my dear viscount, whenever you wish to be regaled with
music come and sup with me.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI have already enjoyed that treat when breakfasting with you,ГўВҖВқ said
Morcerf.

ГўВҖВңDo you mean at Rome?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI do.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, then, I suppose you heard HaydГғВ©eГўВҖВҷs guzla; the poor exile
frequently beguiles a weary hour in playing over to me the airs of her
native land.ГўВҖВқ

Morcerf did not pursue the subject, and Monte Cristo himself fell into
a silent reverie.

The bell rang at this moment for the rising of the curtain.

ГўВҖВңYou will excuse my leaving you,ГўВҖВқ said the count, turning in the
direction of his box.

ГўВҖВңWhat? Are you going?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңPray, say everything that is kind to Countess GГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ” on the part of her
friend the vampire.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd what message shall I convey to the baroness!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat, with her permission, I shall do myself the honor of paying my
respects in the course of the evening.ГўВҖВқ

The third act had begun; and during its progress the Count of Morcerf,
according to his promise, made his appearance in the box of Madame
Danglars. The Count of Morcerf was not a person to excite either
interest or curiosity in a place of public amusement; his presence,
therefore, was wholly unnoticed, save by the occupants of the box in
which he had just seated himself.

The quick eye of Monte Cristo however, marked his coming; and a slight
though meaning smile passed over his lips. HaydГғВ©e, whose soul seemed
centred in the business of the stage, like all unsophisticated natures,
delighted in whatever addressed itself to the eye or ear.

30099m



The third act passed off as usual. Mesdemoiselles Noblet, Julia, and
Leroux executed the customary pirouettes; Robert duly challenged the
Prince of Granada; and the royal father of the princess Isabella,
taking his daughter by the hand, swept round the stage with majestic
strides, the better to display the rich folds of his velvet robe and
mantle. After which the curtain again fell, and the spectators poured
forth from the theatre into the lobbies and salon.

The count left his box, and a moment later was saluting the Baronne
Danglars, who could not restrain a cry of mingled pleasure and
surprise.

ГўВҖВңYou are welcome, count!ГўВҖВқ she exclaimed, as he entered. ГўВҖВңI have been
most anxious to see you, that I might repeat orally the thanks writing
can so ill express.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSurely so trifling a circumstance cannot deserve a place in your
remembrance. Believe me, madame, I had entirely forgotten it.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut it is not so easy to forget, monsieur, that the very next day
after your princely gift you saved the life of my dear friend, Madame
de Villefort, which was endangered by the very animals your generosity
restored to me.ГўВҖВқ

30101m



ГўВҖВңThis time, at least, I do not deserve your thanks. It was Ali, my
Nubian slave, who rendered this service to Madame de Villefort.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWas it Ali,ГўВҖВқ asked the Count of Morcerf, ГўВҖВңwho rescued my son from the
hands of bandits?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo, count,ГўВҖВқ replied Monte Cristo taking the hand held out to him by
the general; ГўВҖВңin this instance I may fairly and freely accept your
thanks; but you have already tendered them, and fully discharged your
debtГўВҖВ”if indeed there existed oneГўВҖВ”and I feel almost mortified to find
you still reverting to the subject. May I beg of you, baroness, to
honor me with an introduction to your daughter?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, you are no strangerГўВҖВ”at least not by name,ГўВҖВқ replied Madame
Danglars, ГўВҖВңand the last two or three days we have really talked of
nothing but you. EugГғВ©nie,ГўВҖВқ continued the baroness, turning towards her
daughter, ГўВҖВңthis is the Count of Monte Cristo.ГўВҖВқ

The count bowed, while Mademoiselle Danglars bent her head slightly.

ГўВҖВңYou have a charming young person with you tonight, count,ГўВҖВқ said
EugГғВ©nie. ГўВҖВңIs she your daughter?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo, mademoiselle,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo, astonished at the coolness and
freedom of the question. ГўВҖВңShe is a poor unfortunate Greek left under my
care.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd what is her name?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHaydГғВ©e,ГўВҖВқ replied Monte Cristo.

ГўВҖВңA Greek?ГўВҖВқ murmured the Count of Morcerf.

ГўВҖВңYes, indeed, count,ГўВҖВқ said Madame Danglars; ГўВҖВңand tell me, did you ever
see at the court of Ali Tepelini, whom you so gloriously and valiantly
served, a more exquisite beauty or richer costume?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңDid I hear rightly, monsieur,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo ГўВҖВңthat you served at
Yanina?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI was inspector-general of the pashaГўВҖВҷs troops,ГўВҖВқ replied Morcerf; ГўВҖВңand
it is no secret that I owe my fortune, such as it is, to the liberality
of the illustrious Albanese chief.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut look!ГўВҖВқ exclaimed Madame Danglars.

ГўВҖВңWhere?ГўВҖВқ stammered Morcerf.

ГўВҖВңThere,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo placing his arms around the count, and
leaning with him over the front of the box, just as HaydГғВ©e, whose eyes
were occupied in examining the theatre in search of her guardian,
perceived his pale features close to MorcerfГўВҖВҷs face. It was as if the
young girl beheld the head of Medusa. She bent forwards as though to
assure herself of the reality of what she saw, then, uttering a faint
cry, threw herself back in her seat. The sound was heard by the people
about Ali, who instantly opened the box-door.

ГўВҖВңWhy, count,ГўВҖВқ exclaimed EugГғВ©nie, ГўВҖВңwhat has happened to your ward? she
seems to have been taken suddenly ill.

ГўВҖВңVery probably,ГўВҖВқ answered the count. ГўВҖВңBut do not be alarmed on her
account. HaydГғВ©eГўВҖВҷs nervous system is delicately organized, and she is
peculiarly susceptible to the odors even of flowersГўВҖВ”nay, there are some
which cause her to faint if brought into her presence. However,ГўВҖВқ
continued Monte Cristo, drawing a small phial from his pocket, ГўВҖВңI have
an infallible remedy.ГўВҖВқ

So saying, he bowed to the baroness and her daughter, exchanged a
parting shake of the hand with Debray and the count, and left Madame
DanglarsГўВҖВҷ box. Upon his return to HaydГғВ©e he found her still very pale.
As soon as she saw him she seized his hand; her own hands were moist
and icy cold.

ГўВҖВңWho was it you were talking with over there?ГўВҖВқ she asked.

ГўВҖВңWith the Count of Morcerf,ГўВҖВқ answered Monte Cristo. ГўВҖВңHe tells me he
served your illustrious father, and that he owes his fortune to him.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWretch!ГўВҖВқ exclaimed HaydГғВ©e, her eyes flashing with rage; ГўВҖВңhe sold my
father to the Turks, and the fortune he boasts of was the price of his
treachery! Did not you know that, my dear lord?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSomething of this I heard in Epirus,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo; ГўВҖВңbut the
particulars are still unknown to me. You shall relate them to me, my
child. They are, no doubt, both curious and interesting.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, yes; but let us go. I feel as though it would kill me to remain
long near that dreadful man.ГўВҖВқ

So saying, HaydГғВ©e arose, and wrapping herself in her burnouse of white
cashmere embroidered with pearls and coral, she hastily quitted the box
at the moment when the curtain was rising upon the fourth act.

ГўВҖВңDo you observe,ГўВҖВқ said the Countess GГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ” to Albert, who had returned to
her side, ГўВҖВңthat man does nothing like other people; he listens most
devoutly to the third act of _Robert le Diable_, and when the fourth
begins, takes his departure.ГўВҖВқ



 Chapter 54. A Flurry in Stocks

Some days after this meeting, Albert de Morcerf visited the Count of
Monte Cristo at his house in the Champs-ГғВүlysГғВ©es, which had already
assumed that palace-like appearance which the countГўВҖВҷs princely fortune
enabled him to give even to his most temporary residences. He came to
renew the thanks of Madame Danglars which had been already conveyed to
the count through the medium of a letter, signed ГўВҖВңBaronne Danglars,
_nГғВ©e_ Hermine de Servieux.ГўВҖВқ

Albert was accompanied by Lucien Debray, who, joining in his friendГўВҖВҷs
conversation, added some passing compliments, the source of which the
countГўВҖВҷs talent for finesse easily enabled him to guess. He was
convinced that LucienГўВҖВҷs visit was due to a double feeling of curiosity,
the larger half of which sentiment emanated from the Rue de la ChaussГғВ©e
dГўВҖВҷAntin. In short, Madame Danglars, not being able personally to
examine in detail the domestic economy and household arrangements of a
man who gave away horses worth 30,000 francs and who went to the opera
with a Greek slave wearing diamonds to the amount of a million of
money, had deputed those eyes, by which she was accustomed to see, to
give her a faithful account of the mode of life of this
incomprehensible person. But the count did not appear to suspect that
there could be the slightest connection between LucienГўВҖВҷs visit and the
curiosity of the baroness.

ГўВҖВңYou are in constant communication with the Baron Danglars?ГўВҖВқ the count
inquired of Albert de Morcerf.

ГўВҖВңYes, count, you know what I told you?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAll remains the same, then, in that quarter?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt is more than ever a settled thing,ГўВҖВқ said Lucien,ГўВҖВ”and, considering
that this remark was all that he was at that time called upon to make,
he adjusted the glass to his eye, and biting the top of his gold headed
cane, began to make the tour of the apartment, examining the arms and
the pictures.

ГўВҖВңAh,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo ГўВҖВңI did not expect that the affair would be so
promptly concluded.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, things take their course without our assistance. While we are
forgetting them, they are falling into their appointed order; and when,
again, our attention is directed to them, we are surprised at the
progress they have made towards the proposed end. My father and M.
Danglars served together in Spain, my father in the army and M.
Danglars in the commissariat department. It was there that my father,
ruined by the revolution, and M. Danglars, who never had possessed any
patrimony, both laid the foundations of their different fortunes.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo ГўВҖВңI think M. Danglars mentioned that in a visit
which I paid him; and,ГўВҖВқ continued he, casting a side-glance at Lucien,
who was turning over the leaves of an album, ГўВҖВңMademoiselle EugГғВ©nie is
prettyГўВҖВ”I think I remember that to be her name.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңVery pretty, or rather, very beautiful,ГўВҖВқ replied Albert, ГўВҖВңbut of that
style of beauty which I do not appreciate; I am an ungrateful fellow.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYou speak as if you were already her husband.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh,ГўВҖВқ returned Albert, in his turn looking around to see what Lucien
was doing.

ГўВҖВңReally,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo, lowering his voice, ГўВҖВңyou do not appear to
me to be very enthusiastic on the subject of this marriage.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңMademoiselle Danglars is too rich for me,ГўВҖВқ replied Morcerf, ГўВҖВңand that
frightens me.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBah,ГўВҖВқ exclaimed Monte Cristo, ГўВҖВңthatГўВҖВҷs a fine reason to give. Are you
not rich yourself?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңMy fatherГўВҖВҷs income is about 50,000 francs per annum; and he will give
me, perhaps, ten or twelve thousand when I marry.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat, perhaps, might not be considered a large sum, in Paris
especially,ГўВҖВқ said the count; ГўВҖВңbut everything does not depend on wealth,
and it is a fine thing to have a good name, and to occupy a high
station in society. Your name is celebrated, your position magnificent;
and then the Comte de Morcerf is a soldier, and it is pleasing to see
the integrity of a Bayard united to the poverty of a Duguesclin;
disinterestedness is the brightest ray in which a noble sword can
shine. As for me, I consider the union with Mademoiselle Danglars a
most suitable one; she will enrich you, and you will ennoble her.ГўВҖВқ

Albert shook his head, and looked thoughtful.

ГўВҖВңThere is still something else,ГўВҖВқ said he.

ГўВҖВңI confess,ГўВҖВқ observed Monte Cristo, ГўВҖВңthat I have some difficulty in
comprehending your objection to a young lady who is both rich and
beautiful.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh,ГўВҖВқ said Morcerf, ГўВҖВңthis repugnance, if repugnance it may be called,
is not all on my side.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhence can it arise, then? for you told me your father desired the
marriage.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt is my mother who dissents; she has a clear and penetrating
judgment, and does not smile on the proposed union. I cannot account
for it, but she seems to entertain some prejudice against the
Danglars.ГўВҖВқ

30107m



ГўВҖВңAh,ГўВҖВқ said the count, in a somewhat forced tone, ГўВҖВңthat may be easily
explained; the Comtesse de Morcerf, who is aristocracy and refinement
itself, does not relish the idea of being allied by your marriage with
one of ignoble birth; that is natural enough.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI do not know if that is her reason,ГўВҖВқ said Albert, ГўВҖВңbut one thing I do
know, that if this marriage be consummated, it will render her quite
miserable. There was to have been a meeting six weeks ago in order to
talk over and settle the affair; but I had such a sudden attack of
indispositionГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңReal?ГўВҖВқ interrupted the count, smiling.

ГўВҖВңOh, real enough, from anxiety doubtless,ГўВҖВ”at any rate they postponed
the matter for two months. There is no hurry, you know. I am not yet
twenty-one, and EugГғВ©nie is only seventeen; but the two months expire
next week. It must be done. My dear count, you cannot imagine how my
mind is harassed. How happy you are in being exempt from all this!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell, and why should not you be free, too? What prevents you from
being so?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, it will be too great a disappointment to my father if I do not
marry Mademoiselle Danglars.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңMarry her then,ГўВҖВқ said the count, with a significant shrug of the
shoulders.

ГўВҖВңYes,ГўВҖВқ replied Morcerf, ГўВҖВңbut that will plunge my mother into positive
grief.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen do not marry her,ГўВҖВқ said the count.

ГўВҖВңWell, I shall see. I will try and think over what is the best thing to
be done; you will give me your advice, will you not, and if possible
extricate me from my unpleasant position? I think, rather than give
pain to my dear mother, I would run the risk of offending the count.ГўВҖВқ

Monte Cristo turned away; he seemed moved by this last remark.

ГўВҖВңAh,ГўВҖВқ said he to Debray, who had thrown himself into an easy-chair at
the farthest extremity of the salon, and who held a pencil in his right
hand and an account book in his left, ГўВҖВңwhat are you doing there? Are
you making a sketch after Poussin?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, no,ГўВҖВқ was the tranquil response; ГўВҖВңI am too fond of art to attempt
anything of that sort. I am doing a little sum in arithmetic.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIn arithmetic?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes; I am calculatingГўВҖВ”by the way, Morcerf, that indirectly concerns
youГўВҖВ”I am calculating what the house of Danglars must have gained by the
last rise in Haiti bonds; from 206 they have risen to 409 in three
days, and the prudent banker had purchased at 206; therefore he must
have made 300,000 livres.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat is not his biggest scoop,ГўВҖВқ said Morcerf; ГўВҖВңdid he not make a
million in Spaniards this last year?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңMy dear fellow,ГўВҖВқ said Lucien, ГўВҖВңhere is the Count of Monte Cristo, who
will say to you, as the Italians do,ГўВҖВ”

      ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҳDenaro e santitГғВ ,

      MetГғВ  della metГғВ .ГўВҖВҷ9


ГўВҖВңWhen they tell me such things, I only shrug my shoulders and say
nothing.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut you were speaking of Haitians?ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo.

ГўВҖВңAh, Haitians,ГўВҖВ”that is quite another thing! Haitians are the _ГғВ©cartГғВ©_
of French stock-jobbing. We may like bouillotte, delight in whist, be
enraptured with boston, and yet grow tired of them all; but we always
come back to _ГғВ©cartГғВ©_ГўВҖВ”it is not only a game, it is a _hors-dГўВҖВҷГ…В“uvre_! M.
Danglars sold yesterday at 405, and pockets 300,000 francs. Had he but
waited till today, the price would have fallen to 205, and instead of
gaining 300,000 francs, he would have lost 20 or 25,000.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd what has caused the sudden fall from 409 to 206?ГўВҖВқ asked Monte
Cristo. ГўВҖВңI am profoundly ignorant of all these stock-jobbing
intrigues.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBecause,ГўВҖВқ said Albert, laughing, ГўВҖВңone piece of news follows another,
and there is often great dissimilarity between them.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh,ГўВҖВқ said the count, ГўВҖВңI see that M. Danglars is accustomed to play at
gaining or losing 300,000 francs in a day; he must be enormously rich.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt is not he who plays!ГўВҖВқ exclaimed Lucien; ГўВҖВңit is Madame Danglars; she
is indeed daring.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut you who are a reasonable being, Lucien, and who knows how little
dependence is to be placed on the news, since you are at the
fountain-head, surely you ought to prevent it,ГўВҖВқ said Morcerf, with a
smile.

ГўВҖВңHow can I, if her husband fails in controlling her?ГўВҖВқ asked Lucien;
ГўВҖВңyou know the character of the baronessГўВҖВ”no one has any influence with
her, and she does precisely what she pleases.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, if I were in your placeГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВқ said Albert.

ГўВҖВңWell?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI would reform her; it would be rendering a service to her future
son-in-law.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHow would you set about it?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, that would be easy enoughГўВҖВ”I would give her a lesson.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA lesson?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes. Your position as secretary to the minister renders your authority
great on the subject of political news; you never open your mouth but
the stockbrokers immediately stenograph your words. Cause her to lose a
hundred thousand francs, and that would teach her prudence.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI do not understand,ГўВҖВқ stammered Lucien.

ГўВҖВңIt is very clear, notwithstanding,ГўВҖВқ replied the young man, with an
artlessness wholly free from affectation; ГўВҖВңtell her some fine morning
an unheard-of piece of intelligenceГўВҖВ”some telegraphic despatch, of which
you alone are in possession; for instance, that Henri IV. was seen
yesterday at GabrielleГўВҖВҷs. That would boom the market; she will buy
heavily, and she will certainly lose when Beauchamp announces the
following day, in his gazette, ГўВҖВҳThe report circulated by some usually
well-informed persons that the king was seen yesterday at GabrielleГўВҖВҷs
house, is totally without foundation. We can positively assert that his
majesty did not quit the Pont-Neuf.ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ

Lucien half smiled. Monte Cristo, although apparently indifferent, had
not lost one word of this conversation, and his penetrating eye had
even read a hidden secret in the embarrassed manner of the secretary.
This embarrassment had completely escaped Albert, but it caused Lucien
to shorten his visit; he was evidently ill at ease. The count, in
taking leave of him, said something in a low voice, to which he
answered, ГўВҖВңWillingly, count; I accept.ГўВҖВқ The count returned to young
Morcerf.

ГўВҖВңDo you not think, on reflection,ГўВҖВқ said he to him, ГўВҖВңthat you have done
wrong in thus speaking of your mother-in-law in the presence of M.
Debray?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңMy dear count,ГўВҖВқ said Morcerf, ГўВҖВңI beg of you not to apply that title so
prematurely.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNow, speaking without any exaggeration, is your mother really so very
much averse to this marriage?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSo much so that the baroness very rarely comes to the house, and my
mother, has not, I think, visited Madame Danglars twice in her whole
life.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen,ГўВҖВқ said the count, ГўВҖВңI am emboldened to speak openly to you. M.
Danglars is my banker; M. de Villefort has overwhelmed me with
politeness in return for a service which a casual piece of good fortune
enabled me to render him. I predict from all this an avalanche of
dinners and routs. Now, in order not to presume on this, and also to be
beforehand with them, I have, if agreeable to you, thought of inviting
M. and Madame Danglars, and M. and Madame de Villefort, to my
country-house at Auteuil. If I were to invite you and the Count and
Countess of Morcerf to this dinner, I should give it the appearance of
being a matrimonial meeting, or at least Madame de Morcerf would look
upon the affair in that light, especially if Baron Danglars did me the
honor to bring his daughter. In that case your mother would hold me in
aversion, and I do not at all wish that; on the contrary, I desire to
stand high in her esteem.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIndeed, count,ГўВҖВқ said Morcerf, ГўВҖВңI thank you sincerely for having used
so much candor towards me, and I gratefully accept the exclusion which
you propose. You say you desire my motherГўВҖВҷs good opinion; I assure you
it is already yours to a very unusual extent.ГўВҖВқ

30111m



ГўВҖВңDo you think so?ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo, with interest.

ГўВҖВңOh, I am sure of it; we talked of you an hour after you left us the
other day. But to return to what we were saying. If my mother could
know of this attention on your partГўВҖВ”and I will venture to tell herГўВҖВ”I am
sure that she will be most grateful to you; it is true that my father
will be equally angry.ГўВҖВқ The count laughed.

ГўВҖВңWell,ГўВҖВқ said he to Morcerf, ГўВҖВңbut I think your father will not be the
only angry one; M. and Madame Danglars will think me a very

are, in fact; one of the oldest of my Parisian acquaintancesГўВҖВ”and they
will not find you at my house; they will certainly ask me why I did not

which shall have a semblance of probability, and communicate the fact
to me by a line in writing. You know that with bankers nothing but a
written document will be valid.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI will do better than that,ГўВҖВқ said Albert; ГўВҖВңmy mother is wishing to go
to the sea-sideГўВҖВ”what day is fixed for your dinner?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSaturday.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThis is TuesdayГўВҖВ”well, tomorrow evening we leave, and the day after we
shall be at TrГғВ©port. Really, count, you have a delightful way of
setting people at their ease.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIndeed, you give me more credit than I deserve; I only wish to do what
will be agreeable to you, that is all.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhen shall you send your invitations?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThis very day.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell, I will immediately call on M. Danglars, and tell him that my
mother and myself must leave Paris tomorrow. I have not seen you,

ГўВҖВңHow foolish you are! Have you forgotten that M. Debray has just seen

ГўВҖВңAh, true.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңFix it this way. I have seen you, and invited you without any

you to accept, as you were going to TrГғВ©port.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell, then, that is settled; but you will come and call on my mother

ГўВҖВңBefore tomorrow?ГўВҖВ”that will be a difficult matter to arrange, besides,
I shall just be in the way of all the preparations for departure.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell, you can do better. You were only a charming man before, but, if
you accede to my proposal, you will be adorable.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat must I do to attain such sublimity?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYou are today free as airГўВҖВ”come and dine with me; we shall be a small
partyГўВҖВ”only yourself, my mother, and I. You have scarcely seen my
mother; you shall have an opportunity of observing her more closely.
She is a remarkable woman, and I only regret that there does not exist
another like her, about twenty years younger; in that case, I assure
you, there would very soon be a Countess and Viscountess of Morcerf. As
to my father, you will not see him; he is officially engaged, and dines
with the chief referendary. We will talk over our travels; and you, who
have seen the whole world, will relate your adventuresГўВҖВ”you shall tell
us the history of the beautiful Greek who was with you the other night
at the Opera, and whom you call your slave, and yet treat like a
princess. We will talk Italian and Spanish. Come, accept my invitation,
and my mother will thank you.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA thousand thanks,ГўВҖВқ said the count, ГўВҖВңyour invitation is most gracious,

not so much at liberty as you suppose; on the contrary, I have a most
important engagement.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, take care, you were teaching me just now how, in case of an
invitation to dinner, one might creditably make an excuse. I require
the proof of a pre-engagement. I am not a banker, like M. Danglars, but
I am quite as incredulous as he is.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI am going to give you a proof,ГўВҖВқ replied the count, and he rang the
bell.

ГўВҖВңHumph,ГўВҖВқ said Morcerf, ГўВҖВңthis is the second time you have refused to
dine with my mother; it is evident that you wish to avoid her.ГўВҖВқ

Monte Cristo started. ГўВҖВңOh, you do not mean that,ГўВҖВқ said he; ГўВҖВңbesides,
here comes the confirmation of my assertion.ГўВҖВқ

Baptistin entered, and remained standing at the door.

ГўВҖВңI had no previous knowledge of your visit, had I?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIndeed, you are such an extraordinary person, that I would not answer
for it.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAt all events, I could not guess that you would invite me to dinner.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңProbably not.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell, listen, Baptistin, what did I tell you this morning when I
called you into my laboratory?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңTo close the door against visitors as soon as the clock struck five,ГўВҖВқ
replied the valet.

ГўВҖВңWhat then?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, my dear count,ГўВҖВқ said Albert.

ГўВҖВңNo, no, I wish to do away with that mysterious reputation that you
have given me, my dear viscount; it is tiresome to be always acting
Manfred. I wish my life to be free and open. Go on, Baptistin.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen to admit no one except Major Bartolomeo Cavalcanti and his son.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYou hearГўВҖВ”Major Bartolomeo CavalcantiГўВҖВ”a man who ranks amongst the most
ancient nobility of Italy, whose name Dante has celebrated in the tenth
canto of _The Inferno_, you remember it, do you not? Then there is his
son, Andrea, a charming young man, about your own age, viscount,
bearing the same title as yourself, and who is making his entry into
the Parisian world, aided by his fatherГўВҖВҷs millions. The major will
bring his son with him this evening, the _contino_, as we say in Italy;
he confides him to my care. If he proves himself worthy of it, I will
do what I can to advance his interests. You will assist me in the work,
will you not?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңMost undoubtedly. This Major Cavalcanti is an old friend of yours,

ГўВҖВңBy no means. He is a perfect nobleman, very polite, modest, and
agreeable, such as may be found constantly in Italy, descendants of
very ancient families. I have met him several times at Florence,
Bologna and Lucca, and he has now communicated to me the fact of his
arrival in Paris. The acquaintances one makes in travelling have a sort
of claim on one; they everywhere expect to receive the same attention
which you once paid them by chance, as though the civilities of a
passing hour were likely to awaken any lasting interest in favor of the
man in whose society you may happen to be thrown in the course of your
journey. This good Major Cavalcanti is come to take a second view of
Paris, which he only saw in passing through in the time of the Empire,
when he was on his way to Moscow. I shall give him a good dinner, he
will confide his son to my care, I will promise to watch over him, I
shall let him follow in whatever path his folly may lead him, and then
I shall have done my part.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңCertainly; I see you are a model Mentor,ГўВҖВқ said Albert ГўВҖВңGood-bye, we
shall return on Sunday. By the way, I have received news of Franz.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHave you? Is he still amusing himself in Italy?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI believe so; however, he regrets your absence extremely. He says you
were the sun of Rome, and that without you all appears dark and cloudy;
I do not know if he does not even go so far as to say that it rains.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHis opinion of me is altered for the better, then?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo, he still persists in looking upon you as the most incomprehensible
and mysterious of beings.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHe is a charming young man,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo ГўВҖВңand I felt a lively
interest in him the very first evening of my introduction, when I met
him in search of a supper, and prevailed upon him to accept a portion
of mine. He is, I think, the son of General dГўВҖВҷГғВүpinay?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHe is.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThe same who was so shamefully assassinated in 1815?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBy the Bonapartists.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes. Really I like him extremely; is there not also a matrimonial
engagement contemplated for him?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, he is to marry Mademoiselle de Villefort.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIndeed?ГўВҖВқ

30115m



ГўВҖВңAnd you know I am to marry Mademoiselle Danglars,ГўВҖВқ said Albert,
laughing.

ГўВҖВңYou smile.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhy do you do so?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI smile because there appears to me to be about as much inclination
for the consummation of the engagement in question as there is for my

Albert rose.

ГўВҖВңAre you going?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңReally, that is a good idea!ГўВҖВ”two hours have I been boring you to death

And your servants, too, how very well behaved they are; there is quite

ГўВҖВңBy all means.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat is not all; give my compliments to your illustrious Luccanese,

ГўВҖВңAh, ha; you will do as much as that, will you?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell, really, nothing is certain in this world.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, count, what a service you might render me! I should like you a

ГўВҖВңNothing is impossible,ГўВҖВқ gravely replied Monte Cristo; and taking leave

Bertuccio appeared.

ГўВҖВңMonsieur Bertuccio, you understand that I intend entertaining company

your services to see that all be properly arranged. It is a beautiful

ГўВҖВңThere must be a good deal done before it can deserve that title, your

ГўВҖВңLet them all be taken away and changed, then, with the exception of

ГўВҖВңI will do everything in my power to carry out your wishes, your

commands concerning the dinner.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңReally, my dear M. Bertuccio,ГўВҖВқ said the count, ГўВҖВңsince you have been in
Paris, you have become quite nervous, and apparently out of your

ГўВҖВңBut surely your excellency will be so good as to inform me whom you

ГўВҖВңI do not yet know myself, neither is it necessary that you should do
so. ГўВҖВҳLucullus dines with Lucullus,ГўВҖВҷ that is quite sufficient.ГўВҖВқ

Bertuccio bowed, and left the room.



 Chapter 55. Major Cavalcanti

Both the count and Baptistin had told the truth when they announced to
Morcerf the proposed visit of the major, which had served Monte Cristo
as a pretext for declining AlbertГўВҖВҷs invitation. Seven oГўВҖВҷclock had just
struck, and M. Bertuccio, according to the command which had been given
him, had two hours before left for Auteuil, when a cab stopped at the
door, and after depositing its occupant at the gate, immediately
hurried away, as if ashamed of its employment. The visitor was about
fifty-two years of age, dressed in one of the green surtouts,
ornamented with black frogs, which have so long maintained their
popularity all over Europe. He wore trousers of blue cloth, boots
tolerably clean, but not of the brightest polish, and a little too
thick in the soles, buckskin gloves, a hat somewhat resembling in shape
those usually worn by the gendarmes, and a black cravat striped with
white, which, if the proprietor had not worn it of his own free will,
might have passed for a halter, so much did it resemble one. Such was
the picturesque costume of the person who rang at the gate, and
demanded if it was not at No. 30 in the Avenue des Champs-ГғВүlysГғВ©es that
the Count of Monte Cristo lived, and who, being answered by the porter
in the affirmative, entered, closed the gate after him, and began to
ascend the steps.

The small and angular head of this man, his white hair and thick gray
moustaches, caused him to be easily recognized by Baptistin, who had
received an exact description of the expected visitor, and who was
awaiting him in the hall. Therefore, scarcely had the stranger time to
pronounce his name before the count was apprised of his arrival. He was
ushered into a simple and elegant drawing-room, and the count rose to
meet him with a smiling air.

ГўВҖВңAh, my dear sir, you are most welcome; I was expecting you.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIndeed,ГўВҖВқ said the Italian, ГўВҖВңwas your excellency then aware of my
visit?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes; I had been told that I should see you today at seven oГўВҖВҷclock.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen you have received full information concerning my arrival?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOf course.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, so much the better, I feared this little precaution might have
been forgotten.ГўВҖВқ

30119m



ГўВҖВңWhat precaution?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThat of informing you beforehand of my coming.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, no, it has not.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңBut you are sure you are not mistaken.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңVery sure.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt really was I whom your excellency expected at seven oГўВҖВҷclock this
evening?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI will prove it to you beyond a doubt.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, no, never mind that,ГўВҖВқ said the Italian; ГўВҖВңit is not worth the
trouble.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, yes,ГўВҖВқ said Monte Cristo. His visitor appeared slightly uneasy.
ГўВҖВңLet me see,ГўВҖВқ said the count; ГўВҖВңare you not the Marquis Bartolomeo
Cavalcanti?ГўВҖВқ
